hard.next_step: Confidence Level 10%. 1 Jul: demo presented to all key stakeholders; some CLM product gaps, Solutions team to get back. Next: share tentative commercials and schedule a technical call to see use cases live.
hard.ais_why: AI momentum from the Tata Play negotiation success story pulled the prospect in; tempered by a practical, cautious team that wants AI to work for multi-stakeholder contracts, inconsistent master data and strategic sourcing. Neha's ask for AI to auto-ingest and recommend clauses from historical contracts is a strong buying signal mapping to a real pain.

north_star_verdict.headline: Live early-stage CLM replacement: the 1 Jul F2F demo engaged Legal, CCO and procurement HODs and the buyer left asking for commercials and a live use-case call, but the win hinges on closing the automated-obligation-tracking gap and naming the unmapped economic buyer.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 24 Jun 2026, qualified 26 Jun, 12 days old at sweep (6 Jul). Close date 24 Jun 2027 = 353 days out. Time-in-Qualified: ~10 days. F2F CLM demo delivered 1 Jul (5 days ago) with Legal, CCO, Business and procurement HODs present; email follow-up 2 Jul. Buyer flagged product gaps yet requested commercials and a technical use-case call as next steps (active pull). No stage slip, no close-date movement. Pace is consistent with an early enterprise CLM motion (discovery to demo to commercials to shortlist), and a 353-day runway supports a full 12-15 month cycle.
evidence: F2F CLM demo delivered 1 Jul 2026 to Legal, CCO, Business and procurement HODs (Next Step 1 Jul; Avoma 1 Jul)
Buyer flagged CLM product gaps but immediately requested tentative commercials and a technical call to see live use cases (Next Step 1 Jul)
Email follow-up on next steps sent 2 Jul (Task 2 Jul, 'Zycus <> VI: F2F meeting next steps')
Multi-threaded engagement: Neha, Shantanu Mallik and Ramdas Prabhu on the 1 Jul call
Close date 353 days out allows a full enterprise CLM cycle; forecast Pipeline is defensible for a Qualified deal with no named economic buyer yet

metrics.narrative: Vodafone Idea's business problem is concrete but not yet quantified: their incumbent SAP Ariba CLM is poorly adopted and used only as a repository for executed contracts, so authoring, redlining and approvals happen outside the system over email and drives, with no obligation tracking across roughly 3,000-5,000 contracts (Tanmay's recap of Neha's six stated challenges, Avoma 1 Jul). The buyer confirmed the pain live: a network-procurement HOD pressed that milestone, SLA and penalty obligations cannot be auto-tracked today (Avoma 1 Jul). No cost of poor adoption, cycle-time loss, compliance exposure or target savings figure has been stated by the buyer; the only number is Zycus's own Telecom Malaysia reference (40% cycle-time reduction on 90,000 contracts). Move: quantify the business case (manual contract risk, compliance/audit exposure, cycle time) with the buyer in the commercial proposal and technical call.
sources: Avoma 1 Jul 2026 (six stated CLM challenges recap)

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is named in Salesforce (only OpportunityContactRole is Shantanu Mallik, Deputy General Manager Supply Chain, with no EB role) and the 26 Jun Opportunity Health task explicitly reads 'Decision Maker is not identified'. The conversation does show where budget authority sits: a senior procurement head who owns IT-digital, agentic and enterprise-business procurement drove the entire commercial and licensing discussion on 1 Jul, pressed on firm pricing for 5,000 contracts and 1,000 users, and framed the initiative as 'a mandate', signalling real budget influence. A ballpark CLM budget of about $100k per year is on record (opportunity Amount). This person is not matched to a Salesforce contact, so we do not assert a name or title. Move: map and name the budget owner and the Legal/CCO approver, then secure a proposal review with them.
sources: SF Opportunity Health task 26 Jun ('Decision Maker is not identified')
Avoma 1 Jul 2026 (procurement head driving pricing; 'it is a mandate')

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria were stated explicitly on 1 Jul: (1) high business-user adoption, because Ariba CLM failed on exactly this; (2) automated obligation and milestone/SLA/penalty tracking, ideally sourced from SAP/ERP and PO consumption; (3) AI-driven risk detection on third-party papers, clause recommendations and contract-versus-contract comparison; (4) integration with SAP, Ariba, ServiceNow, inventory systems and a data lake; and (5) India data residency and LLM/regulatory compliance. The procurement head also asked for a Certinal/iContract differentiation position versus other CLM players. These are confirmed buyer-stated criteria, not inferred. Move: map each criterion to a live use case in the technical call and be explicit where obligation auto-tracking is roadmap-only.
sources: Avoma 1 Jul 2026 (adoption, obligation tracking, InstaReview, integration, India hosting, differentiation ask)

decision_process.narrative: The process visible from the call sequence is early and still forming: a round-table/ground event introduction, an intro meeting 24 Jun, then a F2F CLM demo 1 Jul that pulled in Legal, CCO, Business and network-procurement HODs alongside Supply-Chain procurement, which shows the buyer convening cross-functional stakeholders to evaluate. The buyer's own next steps are commercials plus a technical call to see use cases live, so the near path is commercial proposal then use-case validation. The approval chain (who signs, Legal/CCO/CFO sequencing, procurement mandate ownership) is not documented, and the 24 Jun 2027 close date is a placeholder rather than a buyer-committed decision date. Move: co-author a light mutual evaluation plan naming the decision steps, owners and a target decision date.
sources: SF field history (Initial Interest to Qualified 26 Jun; Opportunity_Source__c Round Table 1 Jul)

paper_process.narrative: This is a new-business, single-module CLM deal (iContract), so the likely contracting path is an Order Form plus a scoped SOW rather than a full-suite MSA, with implementation quoted at roughly 1.75-2x the platform fee and an SOW that Zycus said the delivery team would build after a detailed scoping of integration use cases (Avoma 1 Jul). Nothing on legal review, procurement channel, InfoSec questionnaire, DPA/data-residency paperwork or PO issuance has been discussed yet, which is normal at Qualified. Current_Contract_Expiration for the incumbent Ariba CLM is unknown, so there is no confirmed forcing function. Move: once the technical call lands, get the buyer's procurement/legal contact and the integration use-case list to scope the SOW and surface any InfoSec/security-questionnaire requirement early.
sources: Avoma 1 Jul 2026 (implementation 1.75-2x, SOW via delivery team)

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is well articulated and buyer-owned: Ariba CLM is not adopted and is used only as a repository for executed contracts, so authoring, redlining and negotiation happen outside the system over email; there is poor usability with heavy IT dependency for any configuration; contracts are scattered across email, drives and Ariba with no central OCR-searchable repository; there is no mechanism for obligation, SLA or milestone tracking across roughly 3,000+ contracts; and requests arrive off-system via email/WhatsApp with no trackable SLAs (Tanmay's recap of Neha's six challenges, corroborated live by the procurement HODs, Avoma 1 Jul). The pain owner is procurement/legal contracting leadership (Neha's team plus Supply-Chain and network procurement). This is confirmed pain, not inferred. Move: tie each pain to a proof point in the use-case call and quantify the cost of the status quo.

champion.narrative: The developing champion is Neha, the internal contact who initiated and owns the CLM priority: Tanmay repeatedly credited her ('once Neha reached out', 'Neha priority at this point is for CLM', 'requested by Neha'), and the 2 Jul follow-up email is addressed to her (Task 2 Jul). Shantanu Mallik (Deputy General Manager Supply Chain), the sole formal OpportunityContactRole, and Ramdas Prabhu are the most engaged named contacts, both present on the 1 Jul demo. What has developed: an internal sponsor who convened cross-functional HODs for a F2F and is pulling for commercials. What is missing: no evidence of active advocacy for Zycus over rivals, no introduction to the budget owner or Legal/CCO decision-maker, and no shared internal business case. Move: convert Neha or Shantanu into an active advocate who names the competing CLM vendors and opens the door to the economic buyer.
sources: SF Task 2 Jul (email addressed to Neha)

competition.narrative: The named rival is the incumbent SAP Ariba, whose CLM is the very thing Vodafone Idea wants to replace: it is poorly adopted, used only as a repository for executed contracts, and integration-anchored because SAP is the ERP and 'Ariba is must' for PR/PO integration (Avoma 1 Jul). Ariba's threat is its ecosystem lock-in, not its CLM fit, which is the buyer's stated pain. A 24 Jun intro indicated other CLM vendors are being evaluated, but none were named on the 1 Jul call, so the external competitive field is still a known blind spot. Note: the procurement head's remark that 'Certinal is best for CLM' refers to Zycus's own standalone CLM product, not a competitor, so it is not a rival. Zycus's wedge is native single-codebase AI (conversational adoption, WordConnect, InstaReview risk scoring, contract comparison), India hosting and workflow configurability without IT dependency. Move: name the other CLM vendors and lead with seamless SAP/Ariba integration plus adoption to neutralise the incumbent's lock-in.
sources: Avoma 1 Jul 2026 (Ariba poorly adopted, SAP/Ariba integration must-have)
Avoma 24 Jun 2026 intro (other vendors under evaluation, carried)
Avoma 1 Jul 2026 (Certinal is Zycus's own CLM, not a rival)

deal_movement.summary: Momentum is real and recent: stage moved to Qualified 26 Jun, a multi-HOD F2F CLM demo was delivered 1 Jul, and a next-steps email went out 2 Jul, with the buyer requesting commercials and a technical use-case call. No cooling; last real two-way touch was the 2 Jul email off the 1 Jul demo.

items.change: Stage moved Initial Interest to Qualified (by Tanmay Srivastava)
F2F CLM demo delivered to Legal, CCO, Business and procurement HODs
Buyer requested tentative commercials and a technical call to see use cases live
Next-steps follow-up email sent to Neha and the Vodafone Idea team

competitive_position.summary: SAP Ariba is the single strongest current threat, but as the poorly-adopted incumbent CLM being displaced rather than a preferred rival: SAP is the ERP and Ariba the PR/PO anchor, so its leverage is integration lock-in, not fit (Avoma 1 Jul). A 24 Jun intro flagged other CLM vendors under evaluation, still unnamed, which is the live competitive blind spot to close. No external challenger was named or preferred on the 1 Jul call, and the buyer's 'Certinal is best' remark refers to Zycus's own standalone CLM, not a competitor.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: Ariba CLM is not well adopted... you are just using that for repository for only executed contract. Most of things happening outside the system.
competitors.how_we_win: Lead with native single-codebase AI adoption (conversational UX, WordConnect, InstaReview) and seamless SAP/Ariba integration to turn Ariba's lock-in against it; prove obligation-tracking roadmap credibly.

competitors.name: Other CLM vendors under evaluation (unnamed)
competitors.how_we_win: Name the vendors at the technical call and differentiate on native AI, rapid single-module implementation and India hosting before pricing is benchmarked.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong alignment on adoption, AI risk-detection, integration breadth and India data residency; the one exposed criterion is automated obligation tracking sourced from ERP/PO, which is roadmap-only today.

the entire interface is pretty conversational... user adoption is very high

items.area: Competing CLM vendors under evaluation not named
No quantified ROI / business case for the CLM replacement
items.quote: no buyer-stated cost, savings or cycle-time figure across calls

best_practice_check.summary: Win levers for an early multi-stakeholder CLM replacement: close the obligation-automation objection with a credible product answer, multi-thread to real power, and convert the internal sponsor into an advocate who names the field.
flags: Bring Solutions/Product to the technical call with a concrete SAP/ERP-and-PO obligation-tracking answer; it is the procurement head's #1 objection (1 Jul).
Multi-thread beyond procurement: map and engage the budget owner and the Legal/CCO decision-maker; DM is unidentified (26 Jun health task) and Neha/Shantanu are influencers, not power.
Convert Neha or Shantanu into an active champion who names the competing CLM vendors and drives the internal business case.

items.name: Neha
items.risk: Internal initiator and primary contact but no last name/title on record and no proven access to the budget owner; still an influencer, not confirmed power.
items.source: SF Task 2 Jul (email addressed to Neha); Avoma 1 Jul 2026

items.name: Shantanu Mallik
items.title: Deputy General Manager Supply Chain
items.risk: Sole formal contact role; engaged but reactive, no advocacy or EB introduction yet.
items.source: SF OpportunityContactRole; Avoma 1 Jul 2026; Task 2 Jul

items.risk: Named task/activity contact and present on the demo; title unconfirmed in Salesforce, advocacy unproven.

champion_strength.summary: Developing, not yet strong. Neha initiated and owns the CLM priority (Tanmay: 'requested by Neha'; 2 Jul email addressed to her) and Shantanu Mallik convened the F2F, but there is no evidence of active advocacy for Zycus over rivals, no introduction to the budget owner, and no shared internal business case. Converting Neha or Shantanu into an active advocate is the immediate priority.
champion_strength.source: SF Task 2 Jul (email to Neha); SF OpportunityContactRole (Shantanu Mallik); Avoma 1 Jul 2026

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Zycus led hard and credibly with native single-codebase AI, agentic orchestration, InstaReview risk scoring, clause recommendation and contract comparison, all of which resonated (Neha's clause-recommendation ask; deep LLM/agentic questions on 1 Jul). The one hole is that agentic obligation-tracking from ERP/PO is roadmap-only, so the AI story has a visible gap on the buyer's top criterion.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 7.5 Moderate is corroborated by the call: the buyer probed deeply on which LLMs are used, agentic orchestration, auto-drafting and clause recommendation, and came partly because of AI momentum (Tata Play story). Appetite is genuine but tempered by regulatory, data-residency and LLM-hosting caution, so the field's Moderate rating is accurate, not under-rated.

items.detail: Automated obligation/milestone/SLA tracking sourced from SAP/ERP and PO consumption is roadmap-only ('next 4 months'), the procurement head's core, repeated objection.
Economic buyer and decision-maker unmapped; a skeptical senior procurement head drives commercials but is unnamed in Salesforce.
items.source: SF Opportunity Health task 26 Jun; Avoma 1 Jul 2026

items.detail: Total lands near 3 crore (about 1 crore platform + 1.75-2x implementation); the procurement head pressed hard for firmer, lower pricing.
Ariba CLM died on poor adoption; the same risk recurs unless Zycus proves business-user adoption in the use-case call.

confidence_signals.summary: Engaged and warming: F2F demo 1 Jul, email follow-up 2 Jul, buyer pulling for commercials and a use-case call. No cooling. Watch items are the unresolved obligation-tracking gap and the unmapped economic buyer.

Cross-functional HODs (Legal, CCO, Business, network procurement) joined the F2F (Avoma 1 Jul)

items.expected_effect: Keeps the buyer's pull alive and moves the deal toward shortlisting.

items.action: Book the technical use-case call with Solutions to show obligation tracking, auto-drafting and InstaReview live.
items.trigger: Buyer wants to see use cases live and flagged CLM product gaps
items.expected_effect: Closes the top product objection and proves adoption/fit against Ariba.

items.expected_effect: Establishes CLM credibility and neutralises the 'no financial clients' doubt.

items.expected_effect: Justifies the ~3 crore spend and strengthens the win versus a status-quo Ariba renewal.

critical_signals.text: The 1 Jul F2F CLM demo engaged Legal, CCO, Business and network-procurement HODs and ended with the buyer asking for commercials and a live use-case call, though automated obligation tracking from SAP/ERP was flagged as still missing.
The procurement head asked for auto obligation tracking sourced from SAP/ERP and PO data, plus a Certinal-versus-iContract differentiation one-pager, on 1 Jul.
SAP Ariba is the poorly-adopted incumbent CLM being displaced and the SAP ERP is the integration anchor; no external CLM challenger was named on the 1 Jul call.
Zycus quoted about 1 crore platform plus 1.75-2x implementation for roughly 5,000 contracts and 1,000 users; the price-sensitive procurement head is pressing for firm, lower numbers.
Legal, CCO and network-procurement HODs newly joined the 1 Jul F2F alongside Supply-Chain procurement, and most are not yet individually mapped or engaged.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Vodafone Idea is a live early-stage CLM replacement: the 1 Jul F2F demo engaged Legal, CCO and procurement HODs and the buyer left pulling for commercials and a live use-case call, but the win turns on closing the automated-obligation-tracking gap (ERP/PO-sourced) and naming the still-unmapped economic buyer against a poorly-adopted SAP Ariba incumbent.

win_position.text: We are a credible challenger to replace Vodafone Idea's poorly-adopted SAP Ariba CLM: the 1 Jul F2F demo drew Legal, CCO, Business and procurement HODs, and the buyer left asking for commercials and a technical use-case call (Next Step 1 Jul).

No economic buyer or decision-maker is named (SF health task 26 Jun, 'Decision Maker is not identified'); budget authority sits with an unnamed senior procurement head who called CLM 'a mandate' on 1 Jul but is not yet individually mapped.

The procurement head's core objection is unresolved: automated obligation tracking from SAP/ERP is roadmap-only ('next 4 months'), a fit gap a rival or a status-quo Ariba renewal could exploit (Avoma 1 Jul).
AI is the pull: AIS 7.5 Moderate, and Neha's ask for AI to auto-ingest and recommend clauses from historical contracts maps directly to her pain (Avoma 1 Jul).

deal_momentum.text: Momentum is real and recent: a multi-HOD F2F CLM demo was delivered 1 Jul and followed up by email 2 Jul, with the buyer requesting tentative commercials and a technical use-case call (Next Step 1 Jul).
Seniority and commercials both entered on 1 Jul: pricing (~1 crore platform plus 1.75-2x implementation), licensing, LLM hosting and India data residency were all discussed (Avoma 1 Jul).
No customer-named decision date or close plan yet; the 24 Jun 2027 close is a placeholder and the approval process is still undefined.

customer_commitment.text: The buyer is pulling: they asked to see live use cases, requested a Certinal/iContract differentiation one-pager and firm pricing for about 5,000 contracts and 1,000 users (Avoma 1 Jul).
Commitment is still evaluation-depth, not a shortlist: no exclusivity, no economic-buyer sponsorship, and the other CLM vendors remain unnamed (SF health task 26 Jun, 'keep multi-threaded').

deal_risk.text: Fit risk: obligation auto-tracking from ERP/PO is roadmap-only, and the same 'poor adoption' that killed Ariba CLM could recur if the product gaps aren't closed in the technical call (Avoma 1 Jul).
Price sensitivity: the total lands near 3 crore (about 1 crore platform + 1.75-2x implementation) and the procurement head pressed hard for firmer, lower numbers (Avoma 1 Jul).
Power gap: the economic buyer and decision-maker are unmapped; the single formal contact (Shantanu Mallik) and Neha are influencers, not confirmed power (SF contact roles; 26 Jun health task).

scope_change.detail: Deal was defined as CLM-only from creation (24 Jun); the buyer steering conversation from full S2P back to CLM is scope discipline, not a reduction versus a prior larger scope.

customer_preference.evidence: Buyer engaged deeply on Zycus-specific capabilities and requested commercials, a differentiation one-pager and a live use-case call (Avoma 1 Jul), but did not state a preference over any rival and left an unresolved obligation-tracking product gap.

business_case.evidence: Clear qualitative pain (poor Ariba adoption, scattered contracts, no obligation tracking across ~3,000+ contracts) but no buyer-quantified ROI, savings or cycle-time target; only Zycus's own Telecom Malaysia 40% reference exists (Avoma 1 Jul).
